activeGoal: Execute the mandatory TODO contract injected into ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW: perform one end-to-end live i
designSummary: Issue-driven full-model refinement for TASK-006. No redesign is required. The mandatory TODO injected into ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW is implemented as an executable runtime-validation contract on the existing architecture. Existing ownership remains unchanged: ApplicationFunction ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW stays assigned to software unit {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} / Neo4j Query Native Tool, while implementation may extend the existing repository test harness and listener/result-validation seams to execute one live end-to-end OPENCODE-server request through ThreatIntelPrimary, dynamically parse Neo4j credentials from ELM-TECHNOLOGY-NODE documentation, and validate nondeterministic AI output using structure/contract validation. QA execution remains gated by explicit human confirmation of live_environment_ready.
designDecisions: ADR: Preserve the approved architecture unchanged for TASK-006. The injected TODO on ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW creates a runtime validation obligation, not a need for new ArchiMate structure or new software units.

ADR: Reuse existing software unit {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} / Neo4j Query Native Tool as the primary task owner because the mandatory TODO is attached to its assigned ApplicationFunction. Permit implementation to extend existing test seams in tests/, services/python_listener/, and services/result_assembler/ as supporting realization surfaces rather than introducing a separate QA-only software unit.

ADR: Validate the live OPENCODE-server response with industry-standard structure/contract checks instead of exact-text matching. Reuse the repository's existing json_schema request format and validate_structured_result-based result validation path as the canonical nondeterministic-output acceptance boundary.

ADR: The live end-to-end test execution is architecturally blocked until @QualityAssurance obtains explicit human confirmation of live_environment_ready. This gate constrains test execution only, not implementation of the test harness itself.

issues.summary: TASK-006 live E2E remote message timed out at 120s
issues.details: Validation of commit 6aeb91d81fa848dd951a4a9e344475774a3785f4 reproduced a live-environment defect for TASK-006. The live pytest target tests/test_python_listener.py::test_live_threatintelprimary_e2e_request_uses_graph_derived_neo4j_contract_and_structured_validation ran against THREAT_INTEL_REMOTE_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8124 with live environment readiness confirmed, reached the remote dispatch path, then failed because POST /session/{id}/message timed out after 120.0s. Local tagged contract tests passed, so the remaining defect is remote end-to-end completion under the live server path for REQ-OPENCODE-MULTIAGENT-THREAT-INTEL-001 / ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW / {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394}.

qa.summary: QA failed for TASK-006 on commit 6aeb91d81fa848dd951a4a9e344475774a3785f4. Tagged local automated coverage passed for REQ-OPENCODE-MULTIAGENT-THREAT-INTEL-001, ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW, and {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} in detached worktree validation, including live-environment readiness handling and structured-result contract checks. Live E2E validation actually ran with LIVE_ENVIRONMENT_READY=true and THREAT_INTEL_REMOTE_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8124, but tests/test_python_listener.py::test_live_threatintelprimary_e2e_request_uses_graph_derived_neo4j_contract_and_structured_validation failed because POST /session/{id}/message timed out after 120.0s with RemoteDispatchError, so end-to-end completion remains unverified.

audit.summary: TASK-006 full-model rework audit passed for commit 6328126ff473f3a40f6a3ecc06bb0ba6fb82d05a. Intent model is sufficient with all core layers present and cross-layer relationships established. Reviewed commit stays within existing architecture by changing only tests/test_python_listener.py to extend live E2E timeout tolerance through _resolve_live_remote_timeout_seconds while preserving explicit live_environment_ready gating, graph-derived Neo4j credential loading from ELM-TECHNOLOGY-NODE documentation, ThreatIntelPrimary live request construction, and validate_structured_result-based nondeterministic output validation. Explicit architecture trace evidence remains present for ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW and {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} in the affected tests, and requirement trace REQ-OPENCODE-MULTIAGENT-THREAT-INTEL-001 remains tagged in the same test module. No architecture drift or missing intent-verification evidence was identified in the reviewed scope.
